benefit_summary: Income support of Rs 6,000 per year paid in three equal installments of Rs 2,000 directly to the bank account of eligible landholding farmer families.
documents_required: Aadhaar card|Land ownership records (jamabandi/fard)|Bank account linked to Aadhaar|Mobile number
application_process: Register online at pmkisan.gov.in (Farmer's Corner > New Farmer Registration) or via the nearest Common Service Centre (CSC), then complete e-KYC.
where_to_apply: pmkisan.gov.in / Common Service Centre (CSC) / Village revenue officer (patwari)
notes: Excludes income-tax payers, government employees, pensioners drawing >Rs 10,000/month, and institutional landholders. These exclusions are advisory (not captured in the profile).

benefit_summary: Short-term crop loan at 7% interest, effectively as low as 4% with the prompt-repayment interest subvention. Collateral-free up to Rs 2 lakh. Five-year validity with flexible withdrawal.
documents_required: Application form|Aadhaar/Voter ID/Driving Licence|Address proof|Land holding proof certified by revenue authority|Two passport photos|Cropping pattern with acreage
application_process: Apply at any bank branch or online via the Kisan Rin Portal / Jan Samarth Portal. Security documents needed only above Rs 1.60 lakh.
where_to_apply: Any commercial/cooperative/RRB bank / Kisan Rin Portal
notes: Owner cultivators, tenant farmers, oral lessees, sharecroppers, and SHGs/JLGs are all eligible. Most useful for farmers who do not already hold a KCC.

benefit_summary: Free soil testing and a Soil Health Card reporting fertility and nutrient status with crop-wise fertiliser recommendations. Helps cut fertiliser cost by 20-30% and raise yields by 10-15%. Entirely free of cost.
application_process: Apply online at soilhealth.dac.gov.in or the SHC app (Farmer's Corner), or offline at a local agriculture office / soil-testing lab / CSC; card is issued in 15-30 days after sample collection.
notes: Open to all landholding farmers; small and marginal farmers are prioritised.

benefit_summary: Subsidy for installing a stand-alone solar agriculture pump (up to 7.5 HP). Central + state subsidy covers up to 60% of cost, ~30% bank loan, remainder by the farmer. Especially relevant in Punjab to cut groundwater/energy stress.
documents_required: Aadhaar card|Land records|Bank account|Photograph|Declaration of irrigation need
application_process: Apply through the Punjab Energy Development Agency (PEDA) / state nodal agency portal or the national PM-KUSUM portal; the agency verifies and allots a vendor.
notes: Component B targets individual farmers for stand-alone solar pumps. Component C (grid-connected solarisation) is a separate track.

benefit_summary: Punjab provides zero-cost electricity to agricultural (AP-category) tubewell/pump connections — the only state in India with a full waiver of farm power charges. Covers 14+ lakh agricultural pump connections.
documents_required: Aadhaar card|Agricultural electricity connection (AP) consumer number|Land records|Bank account
application_process: Farmers with an agricultural pump connection are billed at zero; new connections are applied for at the local PSPCL office with land and identity documents.
where_to_apply: PSPCL (Punjab State Power Corporation Ltd) sub-division office
notes: For Punjab farmers who own land with an agricultural tubewell/pump (irrigated) electricity connection.

benefit_summary: 50% subsidy for individual farmers (80% for Custom Hiring Centres / FPOs) on crop-residue machinery — Happy Seeder, Super Seeder, Smart Seeder, Mulcher, Baler, Super SMS — to stop paddy-stubble burning and sow wheat into standing residue.
documents_required: Aadhaar card|Land records (jamabandi)|Bank account|Tractor RC (for mounted machines)|Recent photograph
application_process: Apply online at agrimachinerypb.com within the notified window; department verifies and issues a permission letter before purchase.
notes: For Punjab landowning farmers managing paddy residue; targets the stubble-burning problem. CHCs/FPOs get a higher 80% subsidy.

scheme_name: Crop Diversification Programme - Kharif Maize Incentive (Punjab)
benefit_summary: Cash incentive of Rs 17,500 per hectare for paddy farmers who shift to Kharif maize, to save groundwater and diversify away from water-intensive rice. Expanded to 16 districts for 2026-27.
documents_required: Aadhaar card|Land records|Bank account|Proof of paddy cultivation (girdawari)|Undertaking to sow maize
application_process: Register with the district Agriculture Department under the Crop Diversification Programme before the Kharif season; incentive is paid on verified maize sowing.
where_to_apply: District Agriculture Department (Punjab) / Chief Agriculture Officer
notes: For Punjab paddy growers (owners) willing to switch to maize. Districts include Amritsar, Gurdaspur, Hoshiarpur, Ludhiana, Patiala, Sangrur and others.

benefit_summary: Because Punjab does not implement PMFBY crop insurance, crop-loss compensation is provided under SDRF/NDRF norms — up to about Rs 17,000 per hectare for losses (subject to notified disaster and area limits). This is the state's de-facto insurance substitute.
documents_required: Aadhaar card|Land records|Bank account|Girdawari / crop-loss assessment (special girdawari) by revenue staff
application_process: After a notified natural calamity, the revenue department conducts a special girdawari; eligible farmers receive compensation directly. No prior enrolment needed.
where_to_apply: District Revenue / Deputy Commissioner office (special girdawari)
notes: Punjab has NOT joined PMFBY (opted out; still out as of 2026), so farmers rely on SDRF/NDRF relief for crop loss. Compensation is disaster-triggered, not a premium-based insurance.
